title: The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 10, 2021 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost falls in Ordinary Time, the longest season of the church calendar running from after Pentecost through Advent. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings for this date would have been Amos 5:6-7, 10-15; Psalm 90:12-17; Hebrews 4:12-16; and Mark 10:17-31 (the account of the rich young ruler). This passage, emphasizing Jesus's teaching about wealth and discipleship, would have been paired with Amos's prophetic critique of those who "hate the good and love the evil."

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

In October 2021, the Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of its triennial General Convention held in Baltimore in July, which had approved significant resolutions on racial justice, climate action, and LGBTQ+ inclusion. The Diocese of Colorado was operating under Bishop Laura Ahrens (elected 2015), and the cathedral itself was approaching its 150th anniversary of consecration (1871). No major diocesan controversies or leadership transitions appear to have been active during this specific week.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver weather in early October 2021 was transitional autumn, with typical highs in the 60s-70s Fahrenheit. The Colorado governor's race between Democrat Jared Polis (incumbent) and Republican Heidi Ganahl was underway heading toward the November 2 election. Downtown Denver and Capitol Hill were experiencing post-pandemic reopening dynamics, with restaurants and cultural venues returning to fuller operations after COVID-19 closures. No major local disasters or crises were reported for the immediate week before October 10.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Biden administration was dealing with ongoing inflation concerns and supply chain disruptions as the economy reopened. The House Select Committee investigating the January 6 Capitol riot had just intensified its public hearings and subpoena campaign in late September/early October. The NFL's Denver Broncos season was underway (they were 2-3 at this point in the season), though this would be secondary to national attention focused on political and economic turmoil. Major social divisions over vaccination mandates and COVID-19 policy remained acute in American discourse.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Afghanistan dominated international headlines in early October 2021 as the Taliban government consolidated power following the August U.S. withdrawal, raising urgent humanitarian and refugee concerns. The U.K. was navigating post-Brexit trade complications and fuel shortages. The COP26 climate conference was being scheduled for early November in Glasgow, with escalating international pressure on global emissions commitments and "net zero" targets.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

October 2021 represented a moment of cautious re-emergence in American public life—vaccines were widely available but divisive, offices and schools were reopening, and cultural institutions were reinstating programming. The overall zeitgeist carried lingering pandemic anxiety mixed with political polarization and economic uncertainty. "Succession" Season 3 had premiered on HBO in early October, and sports were returning to fuller attendance. For a cathedral congregation, there would have been ongoing awareness of COVID-19's persistent social and spiritual toll, alongside renewed opportunity for in-person worship and community.
